entries.quote: allow attaching screenshots in feedback — for explaining feature requests or improvements etc
entries.answer: Shipped — you can attach up to 3 screenshots to a feedback or bug report: pick a file, paste, or drag-and-drop straight into the form.

entries.quote: right now, I find it a bit of a hassle to come to the settings in display to change the provider
entries.answer: Shipped — the popover header now has a pin button that puts whichever provider you're viewing into the menu bar instantly. No trip to Settings needed.

entries.quote: I only see the word "DEEP" and I don't see that I can resize this Menu Bar panel
entries.answer: Shipped — the provider row now auto-scrolls so your active provider is always visible, even when the list is longer than the popover.

entries.quote: I have one Xiaomi Mi-mo account submission connected, and I'm thinking if it's possible to add another one or more
entries.answer: Not planned for now. MiMo signs in with a single browser-session login per account, and supporting several at once would need a much larger account and auth rework. We may revisit if there's broader demand.

entries.quote: Claude’s Weekly Limit bar in the Limit Bank section looks better as it is longer and is able to visualise the quota usage much better than Codex’s
entries.answer: Fixed — the Codex 7-Day Usage bar in the Limit Bank now spans the full card width, matching Claude's, so quota is easier to read at a glance.

entries.quote: Would like to have same features to be in Claude Code Desktop
entries.answer: AIMeter is a standalone macOS menu-bar app — it can't add its tracking UI inside Claude Code Desktop. That app is Anthropic's, and there's no extension surface AIMeter could plug into. Happy to revisit if the ask is something AIMeter itself can show.

entries.title: Send feedback without leaving AIMeter
entries.body: Feedback and bug reports now live right in the dashboard sidebar — no GitHub account needed. Everything answered on this page arrived through that form.

entries.title: Auto-borrow on Claude rate limits
entries.body: When a Claude account hits its rate limit mid-request, AIMeter can retry once on your default account's quota so you keep working. Opt-in per account.

entries.title: DeepSeek and Xiaomi MiMo support
entries.body: AIMeter now tracks DeepSeek usage and account balance with a live streaming meter, plus Xiaomi MiMo usage, quota, and night-discount pricing.

entries.title: The big 2.25 update
entries.body: Our first public update since 2.6.5: light and dark mode, Active Sessions, a custom global hotkey, and multi-account Claude routing.

entries.quote: I'm just wondering if it's possible to be able to log in and then it will use my default browser… right now it opens up a pop-up and I have to log in with my Google, and I have to type in my password as well as the 2FA. I can't even use my pass key.
entries.answer: Not possible for cookie-based providers, honestly. AIMeter needs to capture the provider's session cookie to poll your usage, and macOS browsers never expose their cookies to other apps — only an embedded login window can hand that cookie over. Where real OAuth exists (ChatGPT/Codex), AIMeter already opens your default browser. The missing passkey support inside the popup is a limitation of Apple's embedded web view, not a switch the app can flip.

entries.quote: Would it be possible to use the trackpad to swipe and navigate around the providers — two fingers to swipe left and right — instead of clicking the providers one at a time?
entries.answer: Not planned for now. The menubar panel is a small popover, and reliable two-finger gesture navigation there is tricky to get right for what's a niche shortcut — clicking a provider already switches between them. Fun idea though, and thanks for sending it.

entries.quote: Would it be possible to add support for Google's Antigravity provider? I have a Gemini Pro subscription and it would be nice to showcase that here as well.
entries.answer: Shipped in 2.33.0. AIMeter now tracks Google Antigravity (Gemini) quota — sign in with your Google account and your usage shows up grouped just like the Antigravity 2.0 app (weekly + 5-hour limits, expandable to individual models). It does expose a trackable quota surface, so it qualified.

entries.quote: Is it possible to make the control plane description multi-line? Right now it clips the text.
entries.answer: Planned. The description will wrap to multiple lines instead of getting cut off.

entries.quote: Add Antigravity support please for me to use my Gemini Pro sub.
entries.answer: Shipped in 2.33.0 — Google Antigravity (Gemini) quota monitoring with Google sign-in, multiple accounts, and the grouped weekly + 5-hour view (Gemini Models / Claude & GPT models, expandable to each model) matching the Antigravity app.

entries.quote: After I fill in my Google credentials, the login just loads with a blank screen and the window does not close — I cannot get my Xiaomi MiMo subscription logged in.
entries.answer: Fixed in 2.32.0. Google blocks Sign in with Google inside embedded login windows, so it hung on a blank screen. AIMeter now catches that, bounces you back, and points you to Phone/SMS sign-in or the Chrome extension — both work for MiMo.

entries.quote: Cmd+V does not paste the clipboard image into the Feedback / Bug Report section in Send Feedback.
entries.answer: Fixed in 2.31.0. Clipboard images arrive in different formats depending on where you copied them (screenshots are TIFF, Preview/Safari are PNG) and paste only handled the generic type, so it failed for some sources. It now loads whichever format the clipboard actually provides.

entries.title: Google Antigravity (Gemini) quota monitoring
entries.body: AIMeter now tracks your Google Antigravity usage. Gemini and Claude & GPT model quotas are grouped just like the Antigravity 2.0 app — each with a weekly and a 5-hour limit, expandable to the individual models — and you can connect and switch between multiple Google accounts.
